summary: Two-story rectangular Minecraft building with a shallow curved / stepped wooden roof. Exterior walls are layered: lower and upper bands of stonebrick-like blocks with cobblestone corner/column accents, separated by horizontal wooden trim and white smooth block bands. Even rows of glass windows run around both floors. A raised white slab border/ledge surrounds the base on a recessed dirt foundation.

shape.overall_form: rectangular prism with stepped/curved gabled roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along central longitudinal axis (front/back mirrored)

elements: continuous horizontal window rows on both floors (series of 1x1 glass panes)
white smooth slab/strip band between floors and as base border
wooden plank stepped/curved roof with stone slab edging at steps
raised white slab platform/border around building footprint
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint roughly 13x9 blocks (visible roof top ~11x7 inner area plus 1-block white border).
Make two full stories of ~3 blocks interior height each; exterior shows two stacked window rows separated by a 1-block white band and a 1-block wooden trim band.
Use stonebrick blocks for primary wall bands on both floors; place cobblestone at vertical corners/columns.
Place continuous 1-block-high windows (glass pane or glass) running along the midline of each story with regular spacing (about 5 windows on long side).
Add a wooden plank roof built as a stepped curved gable: several layers of full wooden slabs/blocks stepping inwards from both long sides with stone slab/stonebrick edge pieces at the step corners.
Surround base with a 1-block-high white slab border raised one block above surrounding dirt.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white band and base: looks like quartz slab / smooth stone slab; identification not certain (used slab_stone as normalized).
Precise footprint dimensions: top-down images suggest interior roof area and border but exact block counts may vary by ±1-2 blocks.
Interior layout, door placement, and presence of stairs or internal floors are not visible.
Whether windows are glass panes or full glass blocks is ambiguous; they appear as single-thickness panes from distance.
